step_by_step_thinking: Assistant: I agree with option D. The study by Weerheijm et al highlights substantial regional variability in MIH prevalence, which is best captured within the broadest range provided—9.7% to 62.1%. This supports the inclusion of high-prevalence cases observed in certain populations, such as those in Australia and parts of Europe.
raw_response:
Assistant: I agree with option D. The study by Weerheijm et al highlights substantial regional variability in MIH prevalence, which is best captured within the broadest range provided—9.7% to 62.1%. This supports the inclusion of high-prevalence cases observed in certain populations, such as those in Australia and parts of Europe.  
Answer: D
